title: Harden YamlSettingsSource: fix ABC contract, error handling, explicit encoding
analysis: YamlSettingsSource in schema.py (lines 18-64) has three robustness issues: (1) get_field_value returns `None` instead of `tuple[Any, str, bool]` per PydanticBaseSettingsSource ABC, and uses `Any` for the `field` param instead of `FieldInfo`; (2) __call__ has no error handling for corrupt YAML or unreadable files — the `exists()` guard only handles missing files; (3) `open(self.config_path)` uses system-locale encoding instead of explicit UTF-8. The existing test file test_config_schema.py has 8 tests for _expand_env_vars, 3 for FusedMemoryConfig defaults, and type validation tests for sub-configs. The test file uses class-based organization, monkeypatch for env vars, tmp_path for temp files, and direct assertions. All three fixes are localized to schema.py lines 1-64. The test additions will go into new test classes in test_config_schema.py following the existing patterns.

steps.description: Write failing test: get_field_value returns tuple (None, field_name, False) matching ABC contract. Create TestYamlSettingsSourceABCContract class with test_get_field_value_returns_tuple that creates a mock FieldInfo, calls get_field_value, and asserts the return is a 3-tuple (None, field_name, False).

Fix get_field_value ABC contract in schema.py: import FieldInfo from pydantic.fields, change signature to `def get_field_value(self, field: FieldInfo, field_name: str) -> tuple[Any, str, bool]:`, change return to `return (None, field_name, False)`.

Write failing test: corrupt YAML raises RuntimeError. In a new TestYamlSettingsSourceErrorHandling class, write test_corrupt_yaml_raises_runtime_error that writes invalid YAML (e.g. `': :\n  - \x00bad'`) to tmp_path, creates YamlSettingsSource pointing at it, calls source(), and asserts RuntimeError is raised with a descriptive message mentioning the path.

Write failing test: unreadable file raises RuntimeError. Write test_unreadable_file_raises_runtime_error that creates a file in tmp_path, removes read permission with os.chmod(path, 0o000), creates YamlSettingsSource pointing at it, calls source(), and asserts RuntimeError is raised. Skip on Windows. Restore permissions in finally block for tmp_path cleanup.

Add error handling in __call__: wrap the `with open(...)` and `yaml.safe_load()` block in try/except catching (yaml.YAMLError, OSError) and re-raise as RuntimeError with message f'Failed to load configuration from {self.config_path}: {e}'.

Write failing test: UTF-8 content loads correctly. Write test_utf8_yaml_loaded_correctly that writes YAML with non-ASCII characters (e.g. `description: 'Ünïcödé tëst'`) to tmp_path, creates YamlSettingsSource pointing at it, calls source(), and asserts the value is correctly decoded.
Add explicit UTF-8 encoding: change `open(self.config_path)` to `open(self.config_path, encoding='utf-8')` in __call__.

design_decisions.decision: Re-raise as RuntimeError rather than a custom exception
design_decisions.rationale: The task description specifies RuntimeError. The config loading is a startup-time operation and RuntimeError is appropriate for 'this should not happen in a correctly deployed system' failures. Adding a custom exception class would be overengineering for this scope.

design_decisions.decision: Include the file path in the RuntimeError message
design_decisions.rationale: When config loading fails, the operator needs to know which file is problematic. Including self.config_path in the error message makes diagnosis immediate without needing to trace through code.

design_decisions.decision: Catch both yaml.YAMLError and OSError in a single handler
design_decisions.rationale: Both represent 'the file exists but cannot be used as config' — the user action is the same (fix or replace the file). A single except clause keeps the code simple while the chained exception preserves the original cause.

design_decisions.decision: Use FieldInfo import from pydantic.fields (not pydantic)
design_decisions.rationale: pydantic.fields is the canonical location for FieldInfo in pydantic v2. The project already uses pydantic>=2.7 so this is safe. The ABC in pydantic-settings declares the parameter as FieldInfo.

The ABC contract requires tuple[Any, str, bool]. The values mean: no field value resolved (None), the field name as-is (field_name), and not a complex field (False). This is a no-op implementation since resolution happens in __call__, matching the existing comment.

reuse.what: _DummySettings class pattern for isolated YamlSettingsSource testing
reuse.how: New test classes will use the same _DummySettings(BaseSettings) pattern to create YamlSettingsSource instances without needing real config

reuse.what: tmp_path + monkeypatch fixture pattern for YAML file tests
reuse.how: Error handling and UTF-8 tests will write YAML to tmp_path and create YamlSettingsSource pointing at the temp file, same pattern as existing tests
New test classes follow the same class-based pattern with descriptive docstrings
